action.reasoning: Observation: Baseline EER=0.1181 (acc@EER=0.8853) with wav2vec2-base after 5 epochs; loss drops to ~0.004 by epoch 1 (rapid convergence on seen attacks). Model print confirms Wav2Vec2Model + mean-pool + Linear(768->1) head (94M params). Metrics show dev_eer improving then plateauing. Analysis: Simple head + smaller backbone limits OOD generalization to unseen attacks (A07+) and telephony codecs; literature (ASVspoof) favors WavLM-large + stats pooling (mean+std) + MLP with dropout/BN for lower EER. No OOM thanks to max_length=160k + bf16. Time (~18min/iter from history) and 7.6h budget allow ~10-15 more iters with larger model. Decision: Cat current model.py + metrics.jsonl (per critical 'always check workspace before edit' rule) to enable precise incremental edits next; will then switch pretrained='microsoft/wavlm-large', update to StatsPooling+MLP head, lower bs (train=8/dev=32/predict=64), raise epochs=10, keep best-EER checkpoint logic.
action.command:
cat model.py && echo '
=== METRICS ===' && cat logs/iter_8/metrics.jsonl && echo '
=== CURRENT BEST ===' && ls -lh checkpoints/
